title: crataegus azarolus var. heterophylla
intro: Crataegus azarolus var. heterophylla, also known as the Eastern European hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Balkan Peninsula and Eastern Europe, belonging to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects, attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid. The health benefits of Crataegus azarolus var. heterophylla include reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular function, and enhanced immune system response, making it a potential natural remedy for hypertension, atherosclerosis, and other cardiovascular disorders. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health and overall well-being.
